back_translation: When all the kings of the Amorites west of the Jordan, and all the kings of the Canaanites along the coast, heard that the Lord had dried up the waters of the Jordan before the descendants of Israel until they had crossed over, their hearts melted and there was no longer any spirit left in them, because of fear of the descendants of Israel.
At that time the Lord said to Joshua, "Make flint knives and circumcise the descendants of Israel again."
So Joshua made flint knives and circumcised the descendants of Israel at Gibeath-haaraloth.
This is the reason Joshua circumcised them: All the people who had come out of Egypt — namely all the men of war — had died in the wilderness on the way after they had come out of Egypt.
For all the people who had come out had been circumcised, but all the people who were born in the wilderness on the way out of Egypt had not been circumcised.

Because the descendants of Israel walked in the wilderness for forty years, until the whole nation, namely the men of war who had come out of Egypt, had died, since they did not obey the voice of the Lord. The Lord had sworn to them that they would not see the land which He had sworn to their fathers to give us, a land flowing with milk and honey.
He raised up their sons in their place, and these were the ones whom Joshua circumcised, because they had not yet been circumcised, since they had not been circumcised along the way.
When the whole nation had finished being circumcised, they stayed in the camp until they were healed.
The Lord said to Joshua, "Today I have rolled away the reproach of Egypt from you-pl." So that place has been called Gilgal to this day.
The descendants of Israel camped at Gilgal, and on the evening of the fourteenth day of the month they kept the Passover on the plains of Jericho.
On the day after the Passover, on that very day, they ate unleavened bread and roasted grain from the produce of the land.
The day after they had eaten from the produce of the land, the manna ceased. There was no more manna for the descendants of Israel, and that year they ate the produce of the land of Canaan.

Now when Joshua was near Jericho, he lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, a man was standing in front of him with a drawn sword in his hand. Joshua approached him and said to him, "Are you for us or for our enemies?"

He answered, "Neither — for I am the Commander of the army of the Lord. I have now come." Then Joshua fell to the ground on his face and bowed in worship, and said to him, "What does my lord have to say to his servant?"
The Commander of the army of the Lord said to Joshua, "Take off your sandals from your feet, for the place on which you are standing is holy." And Joshua did so.
